items.summary: Add a spinning/tumbling dice roll animation to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. When the Roll Dice button is pressed, the dice element must visually spin (CSS keyframe rotation or 3D tumble) for approximately 0.8–1 second before settling on the rolled value.

Animate player token movement square by square in the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. When a player rolls the dice, their token must visually move one cell at a time along the board path with smooth easing between each step.

Add a sliding down animation to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder when a player lands on a snake head. The token must visually animate downward from the snake's head position to the snake's tail position before the turn ends.

Add a climbing up animation to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder when a player lands on a ladder bottom. The token must visually animate upward from the ladder's base cell to the ladder's top cell before the turn ends.

Add a confetti burst effect to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder when a player wins. When a player's token reaches square 100, a full-screen confetti animation must play over the winner announcement overlay.

Add a glowing turn indicator to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. The current player's name or token label must be highlighted with a neon pulsing glow effect to clearly show whose turn it is. The glow must switch to the other player immediately after a turn ends.

Add Web Audio API sound effects (programmatically generated, no external audio files) to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. Play distinct sounds for: dice roll (short percussive rattle), snake slide (descending pitch tone), ladder climb (ascending pitch tone), and winner fanfare (triumphant ascending chord sequence).

Redesign the player car in the Traffic Car Game at /traffic to look more realistic and detailed. Replace the current basic shape with a multi-layered CSS/canvas car illustration that includes: a car body with visible roof and hood sections, side windows, front windshield, headlights with a glow effect, visible wheels on both sides, and a distinct color scheme that stands out on the road.

Redesign the snake in the Snake Game at /snake to look more realistic and detailed. Replace the current basic rectangle segments with a segmented snake that includes: a distinct head shape with two visible eyes and a tongue, gradient-colored body segments that taper slightly toward the tail, a curved/rounded segment style to give a lifelike serpentine appearance, and a pointed or tapered tail segment.
